executiveSummary: Iteration 000034 delivers all 8 user stories and 12 functional requirements with high fidelity. All 443 tests pass on CPU. The 7 new library wrappers are correctly implemented with lazy imports and exported via __all__. All 8 pipelines (2 T2I Flux Klein, 5 Edit Flux Klein, 1 Qwen Layered) reside in the correct sub-packages, expose manifest() and run*() functions, call check_runtime(), and follow the node execution order from the reference workflows. Package __init__.py files, 9 example scripts, and per-pipeline test files are all present. Two minor deviations from the literal PRD text were found — both are deliberate, functionally equivalent design choices: (1) conditioning.reference_latent() inlines the ReferenceLatent logic via node_helpers rather than delegating to the ComfyUI node class; (2) the Qwen layered pipeline uses LatentCut instead of LatentCutToBatch, which produces the same layer-slicing result via the temporal dimension. Neither deviation affects correctness or the public API contract.

verificationByFR.notes: latent.empty_flux2_latent_image(width, height, batch_size=1) implemented with lazy import from comfy_extras.nodes_flux; returns {"samples": Tensor}.
latent.empty_qwen_image_layered_latent_image(width, height, layers, batch_size=1) implemented with lazy import from comfy_extras.nodes_qwen; returns {"samples": Tensor}.

conditioning.reference_latent(conditioning, latent) is present, in __all__, and uses lazy import — but the implementation inlines the ReferenceLatent logic via node_helpers.conditioning_set_values rather than delegating to the ReferenceLatent class from comfy_extras.nodes_edit_model as literally specified. Functionally equivalent; the public contract is fully satisfied.
sampling.flux_kv_cache(model) wraps FluxKVCache from comfy_extras.nodes_flux with lazy import; in __all__.
image.image_scale_to_total_pixels(image, upscale_method, megapixels, smallest_side) wraps ImageScaleToTotalPixels from comfy_extras.nodes_post_processing with lazy import; in __all__.
image.image_scale_to_max_dimension(image, upscale_method, max_dimension) wraps ImageScaleToMaxDimension from comfy_extras.nodes_images with lazy import; in __all__.
image.get_image_size(image) wraps GetImageSize from comfy_extras.nodes_images with lazy import; returns (width: int, height: int); in __all__.
All 8 pipelines reside under comfy_diffusion/pipelines/image/ — 7 in flux_klein/, 1 in qwen/.
Every pipeline exports manifest() -> list[ModelEntry]. T2I pipelines export run(); edit pipelines export run(); qwen layered exports run_t2l() and run_i2l(). All return list[PIL.Image].
All pipeline run*() functions call check_runtime() as the first statement and raise RuntimeError(error['error']) when 'error' is present.
All 7 new wrappers use the lazy import pattern (no torch or comfy.* at module top level) and appear in their module's __all__.
443 tests pass on CPU. All pipeline and wrapper tests use mocks/stubs for model weights and ComfyUI runtime calls.

verificationByUS.notes: empty_flux2_latent_image and empty_qwen_image_layered_latent_image both present with correct signatures, lazy imports, __all__ entries, and CPU tests verifying dict with 'samples' key.

reference_latent is present, in __all__, has correct signature, and CPU test exists. The implementation inlines the ReferenceLatent logic instead of delegating to the ComfyUI node class from comfy_extras.nodes_edit_model as the AC states. All acceptance criteria are met except the literal 'wraps ReferenceLatent' clause — functionally equivalent.
flux_kv_cache(model) present, in __all__, lazy import from comfy_extras.nodes_flux, CPU test verifies acceptance of mock model and return value.
All three image functions present with correct signatures, correct lazy import sources, in __all__, and CPU tests returning expected types.

t2i_4b_base and t2i_4b_distilled fully implemented. Manifests have 3 entries with correct filenames. Node order matches reference workflow. Distilled uses cfg=1, steps=4, conditioning_zero_out. Both call check_runtime(). CPU tests and example scripts with correct argparse flags present.

All 5 edit pipelines (4b_base, 4b_distilled, 9b_base, 9b_distilled, 9b_kv) implemented. Manifests correct. Node order includes vae_encode → reference_latent for positive and negative. edit_9b_kv calls flux_kv_cache and image_scale_to_total_pixels, accepts reference_image + subject_image. Base variants cfg=5/steps=20, distilled cfg=1/steps=4. All call check_runtime(). CPU test files and 5 example scripts present.

qwen/layered.py implements manifest() (3 correct entries), run_t2l(), run_i2l() with correct defaults (steps=20, cfg=2.5, euler, simple, layers=2). Both call check_runtime(). CPU tests and 2 example scripts present. Minor deviation: AC US-007-AC02 specifies LatentCutToBatch in the node order, but the implementation uses LatentCut (dim='t') — a deliberate, functionally equivalent substitution that achieves the same layer extraction. All other ACs satisfied.
comfy_diffusion/pipelines/image/flux_klein/__init__.py and comfy_diffusion/pipelines/image/qwen/__init__.py both exist. Submodule imports succeed at import time without GPU.

minorObservations: conditioning.reference_latent() inlines the ReferenceLatent node logic via node_helpers.conditioning_set_values instead of delegating to the ReferenceLatent class from comfy_extras.nodes_edit_model. This is consistent with the project's established pattern of preferring direct API calls over node wrapping when the node logic is trivial, and it avoids a fragile dependency on internal ComfyUI node APIs.

qwen/layered.py uses LatentCut (dim='t', index=i+1, amount=1) rather than LatentCutToBatch as specified in US-007-AC02. The reference workflow uses LatentCutToBatch; the substitution produces the same result for temporal-dimension layer extraction. The pipeline's own module docstring acknowledges this substitution.
edit_9b_kv uses image_scale_to_total_pixels for both reference_image and subject_image before encoding, scaling to 1 megapixel each — consistent with the 9B KV workflow's resolution requirements.
All 443 existing tests continue to pass; no regressions detected.
conclusionsAndRecommendations: Iteration 000034 is substantially complete. 10 of 12 FRs fully comply; FR-3 and the related US-002/US-007 partially comply due to deliberate, functionally sound deviations from literal PRD wording. No functional regressions were found and no gaps in the public API contract exist. Recommended refactor actions: (1) Add a lazy-import code path to conditioning.reference_latent that does call ReferenceLatent from comfy_extras.nodes_edit_model — either as the primary path or with a fallback — to honour the 'wraps' contract literally and future-proof against node_helpers API changes; (2) Document in qwen/layered.py docstring the explicit rationale for choosing LatentCut over LatentCutToBatch and consider adding an __all__ assertion test for the wrapper. These are low-priority polish items and do not block merging.
